qa_pairs.question: How do I submit a multinode distributed parallel Ansys CFX job on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit a multinode distributed parallel Ansys CFX job on an Alliance cluster, you'll need a Slurm script. This script should load `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2023R2` (or a newer Ansys module version). The core command is `cfx5solve -def YOURFILE.def -start-method "MPI Distributed Parallel" -par-dist $NNODES`, where `$NNODES` is determined by `slurm_hl2hl.py --format ANSYS-CFX`. Key Slurm directives in the script include specifying the account (`--account`), time limit (`--time`), number of compute nodes (`--nodes`, set to 2 or more for multinode), tasks per node (`--ntasks-per-node=192` to use all cores), and memory (`--mem=0` to use all memory).

qa_pairs.question: What are the specific MPI `start-method` configurations for running Ansys CFX in a distributed parallel mode on different Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: When running Ansys CFX in distributed parallel mode on Alliance clusters, the `start-method` in the `cfx5solve` command depends on the cluster. For Narval or Fir clusters, you should use `"Open MPI Distributed Parallel"`. For other Alliance clusters, the `start-method` is `"Intel MPI Distributed Parallel"`, and you must also set the environment variable `export I_MPI_HYDRA_BOOTSTRAP=ssh` and unset `I_MPI_HYDRA_BOOTSTRAP_EXEC_EXTRA_ARGS`.
